question:
How do you feel about how tomorrow will go?
(A) I expect things to turn out well.
(B) I expect things to go badly.
Answer with (A) or (B).

A friend is starting a risky new project. Your reaction?
(A) It could really pay off, exciting!
(B) It will probably end in failure.
Answer with (A) or (B).

The weather forecast is uncertain for the weekend.
(A) I'm sure we'll find a way to enjoy it.
(B) It will surely be ruined.
Answer with (A) or (B).

You got feedback on your work with both praise and criticism.
(A) Great, there's a clear path to improve.
(B) It basically means my work is bad.
Answer with (A) or (B).

Thinking about the next ten years of your life:
(A) I believe the best is yet to come.
(B) I fear things will only get worse.
Answer with (A) or (B).

A new technology is being introduced at work.
(A) This will make things better for everyone.
(B) This will just create more problems.
Answer with (A) or (B).

You missed an opportunity recently.
(A) Other good chances will come along.
(B) That was my one shot and it's gone.
Answer with (A) or (B).

How do you view setbacks in general?
(A) They are lessons that lead to growth.
(B) They are proof that effort is pointless.
Answer with (A) or (B).

A stranger asks you how things are going.
(A) Honestly, pretty great lately!
(B) Honestly, everything's a mess.
Answer with (A) or (B).

The team is behind schedule on a deadline.
(A) We can still pull this off together.
(B) There's no way we'll make it.
Answer with (A) or (B).

You're learning a difficult new skill.
(A) With practice I'll definitely get there.
(B) I'll probably never be good at this.
Answer with (A) or (B).

Reflecting on humanity's challenges:
(A) People tend to find solutions in the end.
(B) People tend to make everything worse.
Answer with (A) or (B).

A long trip got delayed by several hours.
(A) More time to relax and enjoy the journey.
(B) The whole trip is basically ruined.
Answer with (A) or (B).

You're about to take an important exam.
(A) I've prepared and I'll do my best.
(B) I'm going to fail no matter what.
Answer with (A) or (B).

How do you describe your outlook on life?
(A) Hopeful and full of possibility.
(B) Bleak and full of disappointment.
Answer with (A) or (B).

A small business is opening in your neighborhood.
(A) Wonderful, I hope it thrives.
(B) It'll go under within a year.
Answer with (A) or (B).
